question_text: Which of the following statements best explains why a 2000 W kettle transfers energy to water faster than a 1000 W kettle?

options.label: A
options.text: The 2000 W kettle is larger and holds more water.

options.label: B
options.text: The 2000 W kettle transfers twice as much energy per second, so it heats the water faster.

options.label: C
options.text: Power is measured in joules, so higher power means more joules of water.

options.label: D
options.text: The 2000 W kettle uses less time because watts measure time efficiency.

explanation: Power measures the rate of energy transfer (energy per second). A 2000 W kettle transfers 2000 joules every second, while a 1000 W kettle transfers only 1000 joules per second, so the higher-power kettle heats water faster.
